questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following regions in India is known for Tropical Evergreen Forests?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Tropical Evergreen Forests are found in the Western Ghats.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.subTopic: Prime Minister Chief Minister Council of Ministers
questions.question: Which of the following is the correct descending order of ministerial ranks in the Union Council of Ministers, as per Indian practice?
options: Cabinet Minister to Minister of State to Deputy Minister
Cabinet Minister to Minister of State to Deputy Minister
Cabinet Minister to Minister of State to Deputy Minister
Cabinet Minister to Minister of State to Deputy Minister
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The correct descending order is Cabinet Minister, Minister of State, Deputy Minister.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Bhabar region lies in which part of the Great Plains?
options: Adjacent to the foothills of the Himalayas
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Bhabar region lies adjacent to the foothills of the Himalayas.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following important Indian festivals was observed on 13th and 14th March 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Holi was observed on 13th and 14th March 2025.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which issue did the World Economic Forum highlight as the top immediate global threat in its 2025 Global Risks Report published in January 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. State-based armed conflict was highlighted as the top immediate global threat.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Geographically, Bhutan is divided into four regions. Which of the following is NOT a name of those divisions?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Northern is not a geographical division of Bhutan.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Parthians originated from which of the following countries?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The Parthians originated from Iran.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who among the following was honoured as the International Cricket Council (ICC) Men's Cricketer of the Year for outstanding performance in 2024?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Jasprit Bumrah was honoured as ICC Men's Cricketer of the Year for 2024.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which technology uses acoustic waves to locate objects in the ocean?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Sonar uses acoustic waves to locate objects in the ocean.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: India's first exploration licence auction and AI-Driven Mineral Targeting Hackathon was launched in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The launch took place in Goa.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What is the name of the patented respiratory device developed by AIIMS Raipur to curb airborne infections in May 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. HOAC Combo is the patented respiratory device developed by AIIMS Raipur.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In post 1991 reforms, the term 'disinvestment' refers to:
options: selling government's share in public sector companies
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Disinvestment refers to selling government's share in public sector companies.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following acts introduced the Federal Structure in British India for the first time?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The Government of India Act, 1935 introduced the Federal Structure.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which is a landmark judgement of Justice Bela Trivedi, whose official retirement is scheduled for June 2025?
options: Challenge to the Abrogation of Article 370
Euthanasia and the Right to Die with Dignity
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Justice Bela Trivedi delivered the landmark judgement on the challenge to the abrogation of Article 370.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In the Pallava administration, which was the smallest administrative unit directly managed by local self-governing assemblies?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Ur was the smallest administrative unit in Pallava administration.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of acids?
options: They release OH ions in water.
They react with bases to form salt and water.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Acids release H+ ions, not OH- ions.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Narayana Guru, a social reformer who led a massive movement against the discrimination of the lower castes, was from the state of:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Narayana Guru was from Kerala.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Why can Gross Enrollment Ratio (GER) sometimes exceed 100%?
options: Due to school mergers in rural areas
Due to inclusion of post-graduate students
Due to enrollment of learners outside the official age group
Because of errors in census data
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. GER can exceed 100% due to enrollment of learners outside the official age group.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following actions will permanently delete a file from an MS Windows system, bypassing the Recycle Bin?
options: Dragging the file to the Recycle Bin
Right-clicking on the file and selecting 'Delete'
Selecting the file and pressing Shift + Delete keys
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Shift + Delete keys permanently delete a file, bypassing the Recycle Bin.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which city is constructing a 27-metre-tall clock tower at Talkatora Roundabout to enhance its architectural identity?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. New Delhi is constructing a 27-metre-tall clock tower at Talkatora Roundabout.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which key combination is commonly used in Windows OS to open the Task Manager?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Ctrl + Shift + Esc opens the Task Manager in Windows.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: From the 11th through the 18th centuries, what type of literature was the most prominent in medieval India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Devotional poetry was the most prominent literature in medieval India.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following years was the Right to Information Act passed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Right to Information Act was passed in 2005.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The 'Kundara Proclamation' which openly called for taking up arms against the British, is associated with which of the following freedom fighters?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The Kundara Proclamation is associated with Velu Thampi.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following years did Vodafone start its journey in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Vodafone started its journey in India in 2007.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which state is leading with the highest number of solar power installations under PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana (PMSGMBY) by March 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Maharashtra is leading with the highest number of solar power installations.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which company received the 'Champion Company' award at the inaugural CII Awards for Excellence in Disability Inclusion in 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Wipro Limited received the 'Champion Company' award.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In April 2025, the Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs has approved the development of a Greenfield High-Speed Corridor connecting which two states on NH-06 under Hybrid Annuity Mode (HAM)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The corridor will connect Manipur and Mizoram.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Under Article 356 of the Indian Constitution, President's Rule can be imposed in a state on the recommendation of the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. President's Rule is imposed on the recommendation of the State Government.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In May 2025, who was appointed as Secretary, Department of Revenue, Ministry of Finance?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Smarak Swain was appointed as Secretary, Department of Revenue.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: On 21 May 2025, the Director General of Health Services (DGHS) in Delhi was appointed to oversee the implementation of which two major healthcare initiatives?
options: PM-JAY and National Digital Health Mission
Fit India Movement and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan
Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission and Ayushman Arogya Mandir
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. DGHS will oversee Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission and Ayushman Arogya Mandir.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following years was the Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY) launched by the Government of India to provide connectivity to unconnected habitations as part of a poverty reduction strategy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. PMGSY was launched in 2000.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: When was the Basel Convention adopted on the Control of Transboundary Movements of Hazardous Wastes and their Disposal?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Basel Convention was adopted in 1989.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: To pass a Constitutional Amendment Bill in the Lok Sabha, which of the following correctly states the minimum support required?
options: Two-thirds majority of the total membership of the House
Simple majority of Lok Sabha members and two-thirds of Rajya Sabha members present and voting
Unanimous vote of all Members of Parliament
Majority of the total membership and two-thirds of members present and voting
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The bill requires a majority of total membership and two-thirds of members present and voting.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Where is the headquarters of the North Western Railway zone located?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The headquarters of North Western Railway is in Jaipur.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The region between the Tropic of Cancer and the Tropic of Capricorn is known as the:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The region between the Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn is the Tropical zone.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: According to the PLFS Annual Report (July 2023-June 2024), which Indian state made history in 2025 by becoming the first to achieve full literacy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Himachal Pradesh became the first state to achieve full literacy.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following was the first state to be formed on linguistic basis after severe agitation and the death of a protester?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Andhra Pradesh was the first state formed on linguistic basis.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following groups does hydrogen belong?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Hydrogen belongs to Group 1 of the periodic table.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The Bhabar region lies in which part of the Great Plains?
options: Adjacent to the foothills of the Himalayas
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Bhabar region lies adjacent to the foothills of the Himalayas.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Train A leaves station M at 7:50 a.m. and reaches station N at 2:50 p.m. on the same day. Train B leaves station N at 9:50 a.m. and reaches station M at 2:50 p.m. on the same day. Find the time when Trains A and B meet.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The trains meet at 11:55 a.m.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: What smallest number should be added to 4519 so that the sum is completely divisible by 30?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. 11 should be added to 4519 to make it divisible by 30.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The product of two positive numbers is 2880. If the first number is five times of the second number, then the sum of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The sum of the two numbers is 144.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The population of a district is 351000, out of which 164000 are males. 14% of the population is literate. If 14% males are literate, then what percentage of females are literate?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. 14 percent of females are literate.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Two numbers have HCF 6 and LCM 36. If the difference between the two numbers is 30, what are the two numbers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The two numbers are 6 and 36.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Sapna invested 20,900 on simple interest, partly at 10% per annum and partly at 9% per annum. If she earns equal interests from the two investments after 5 years, then find the sum invested at 10% per annum.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The sum invested at 10 percent is 9,900.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Simplify: 3(((4/3)x^2) - 30x + 18) - 4(x^2 + 8x - 14)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Simplifying gives -122x + 110.

questions.question: Which of the following ratios is the greatest?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. 40:51 is the greatest ratio.

questions.question: Sakshi bought a toy for 456, decorated it at a cost of 20 and sold the toy for 612. Find the loss or profit percentage.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Sakshi made a profit of 200/7 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. A alone can complete the work in 28,275 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If 2 times the mother's age is 26 years more than 4 times her daughter's age, and 3 times the daughter's age is 2 years less than the mother's age, then what is the difference (in years) between the ages of the mother and the daughter?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The difference between their ages is 25 years.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: ABCD is a trapezium in which BC || AD and AC = CD. If angle ABC = 18 degrees and angle BAC = 93 degrees, then what is the measure of angle ACD (in degree)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The measure of angle ACD is 51 degrees.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Given that 4^0.72 = x, 4^0.86 = y and x^2 = y^z, then the value of z is close to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value of z is close to 9.56.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The average weight (in kg) of a family of five members whose weights are 40 kg, 49 kg, 56 kg, 75 kg and 39 kg is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The average weight is 51.8 kg.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The marked price of a stainless-steel bottle is ₹1,200. After applying two successive discounts of 10% and 5%, what is the selling price?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The selling price is 1,026.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: An equilateral triangle has each side measuring 13 cm; find its perimeter.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The perimeter is 39 cm.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Anjali can do a certain piece of work in 30 days. Anjali and Ayushi can together do the same work in 18 days, and Anjali, Ayushi and Ankita can do the same work together in 9 days. In how many days can Anjali and Ankita do the same work together?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Anjali and Ankita can do the work together in 45 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A can lay railway track between two given stations in 19 days and B can do the same job in 16 days. With the help of C, they did the job in 5 days only. Then, C alone can do the job in
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. C alone can do the job in 11 101/129 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.subTopic: Part to Part and Part to Whole
questions.question: A, B and C invest a sum in the ratio of 63 : 35 : 42, respectively. If they earned a total profit of 5,120 at the end of the year, then what is the difference between share of B and C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The difference between shares of B and C is 256.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The number 245015 is divisible by which of the following?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. 245015 is divisible by 15.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the circumference (in m) of the largest circle that can be drawn completely inside a rectangle whose dimensions are given as 126 m and 226 m. Take pi = 22/7.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The circumference is 396 m.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A man goes to Lucknow from Kanpur at a speed of 72 km/hr and returns to Kanpur at speed of 90 km/hr, through the same route. What is his average speed (in km/hr) of the entire journey?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The average speed is 80 km/hr.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Simplify: sec^2 beta - 1/(cosec^2 beta - 1)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The simplified value is 1.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Left) 1 & 94 5#62 82$ @&#5%&#1 (Right) How many such symbols are there each of which is immediately preceded by a number and also immediately followed by a symbol?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. There are 2 symbols that meet the condition.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, J, K and L are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre of the table. L sits to the immediate right of D. Only three people sit between L and C when counted from the left of L. Only three people sit between D and J. A sits to the immediate right of K. How many people sit between D and K when counted from the right of K?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Four people sit between D and K when counted from the right of K.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. IN-JG does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 3 is added to each odd digit and 1 is subtracted from each even digit in the number 6243514, what will be the difference between the highest and lowest digits in the number thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The difference between the highest and lowest digits is 6.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 84, 85, 87, 91, 99, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The next number in the series is 115.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 'A' stands for '/', 'B' stands for 'x', 'C' stands for '*' and 'D' stands for '-', then what will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation? (117 A 3) C (119 A 7) C 24 D 6 C (117 A 13) B 2 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The value is 98.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What will come in the place of the question mark (?) in the following equation, if '+' and 'x' are interchanged and '-' and '+' are interchanged? 25 / 6 - 8 + 20 x 4 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value is 152.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'LEFT' is coded as '5427' and 'EARS' is coded as '6153'. What is the code for 'E' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The code for 'E' is 6.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Rajeev starts from Point A and drives 8 km towards east. He then takes a left turn, drives 4 km, turns left and drives 4 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 11 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 4 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Rajeev should drive 7 km towards north.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? COB 5, EQD 10, GSF 15, IUH 20, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. KWJ 25 will replace the question mark.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'A = B' means 'A is the wife of B', 'A # B' means 'A is the father of B', 'A # B' means 'A is the brother of B' and 'A % B' means 'A is the mother of B'. How is D related to A if 'D % E = L # T # A'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. D is the mother's mother of A.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statement(s). Statements: All meters are compasses. All compasses are scales. Conclusions: (I) Some scales are compasses. (II) All meters are scales.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Both conclusions (I) and (II) follow.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (Note: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g., 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding/subtracting/multiplying to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) (75, 62, 49) (61, 48, 35)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. (92, 79, 66) follows the same pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: ETHC is related to XOMJ in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, CZBE is related to VUGL. To which of the given options is HKQZ related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. HKQZ is related to AFVG.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which one DOES NOT belong to that group? (NOTE: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. KE-MI does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: CFJP is related to GJMS in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, SVVB is related to WZYE. To which of the given options is EHEK related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. EHEK is related to LIHN.

(Left) 5#%2^3 8/7+52#6/19 (Right) How many symbols in the series are immediately preceded by a number and immediately followed by a symbol?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. There is 1 symbol that meets the condition.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: Some kites are hats. No hat is a bat. Conclusions: (I): Some kites are bats. (II): Some hats are kites.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Only conclusion (II) follows.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. UDB 159 will replace the question mark.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Nushrat ranked 12th from the bottom and 21st from the top in his class. How many students are there in his class?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. There are 32 students in the class.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 81374569 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. The position of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. One digit will remain unchanged.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. GC 13 is related to JF 18.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. Only one person sits between C and D when counted from the right of C. Only one person sits between D and A. Only one person sits between C and B. Only two people sit between F and A. Only one person sits between E and B. How many people sit between E and G when counted from the left of G?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Two people sit between E and G when counted from the left of G.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Amit ranked 34th from the top and 45th from the bottom in his class. How many students are there in his class?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. There are 78 students in the class.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Town B is to the south of Town A. Town C is to the west of Town A. Town D is to the west of Town C. Town E is to the north of Town D and also to the south of Town F. What is the position of Town F with respect to Town D?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Town F is to the north of Town D.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 86 88 92 100 116 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The next number in the series is 148.

Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The next number in the series is 57.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
